assertion.summary: Feature-owned proof mapping for the real kill-worker lifecycle. It ties the new checked-in Mission-GUI-TEST Electron flow artifact for Mission session `69792bf9-59b0-4933-a873-43c546853bc1` to the persisted `worker_failed(reason = "Killed by user") -> mission_paused` timeline, same-session paused/resumable UI screenshots, and focused automated coverage for `droid.kill_worker_session` plus Mission kill-worker semantics.

automated.case: Mission Control timeline distinguishes user kill and daemon failure events
automated.verifies: Mission progress renders `Worker killed by user` followed by `Mission paused`, proving the runtime timeline distinguishes `worker_failed(reason = "Killed by user") -> mission_paused` from daemon failure.

automated.case: kill messaging distinguishes user-killed workers from daemon failures and acknowledges pending kill
automated.verifies: The UI immediately acknowledges Kill Worker with pending feedback, then exposes paused-after-user-kill recovery copy saying the worker was terminated at the user's request, leaving the Mission inspectable and resumable.

automated.case: mission session plumbing preserves agi/orchestrator settings and supports kill_worker_session
automated.verifies: The real JSON-RPC request path sends `droid.kill_worker_session` for the active Mission session and preserves Mission metadata before and after the worker-kill operation.

interactive.evidence: This feature's new flow artifact ties fresh Mission-GUI-TEST Electron screenshots (`kill-proof-after-reload.png`, `kill-proof-mission-control.png`, and `kill-proof-chat-resumable.png`) to the exact same selected Mission session `69792bf9-59b0-4933-a873-43c546853bc1`, its isolated session file, and the persisted missionDir progress/state files that record the real user-kill transition.

Fresh feature-owned setup capture shows Mission-GUI-TEST with Mission mode selected and model `CCH-GPT-5.4` visible before send, proving this kill-worker proof stayed on the required Electron surface and model constraint instead of reusing only older milestone screenshots.

The Mission Control screenshot for session `69792bf9-59b0-4933-a873-43c546853bc1` shows `Paused`, `Worker killed by user`, and `Worker: No active worker` while the same sidebar session remains selected. The paired `kill-proof-chat-resumable.png` screenshot in the new flow artifact shows the same session's composer enabled, proving the real killed Mission remains inspectable and resumable on that same session.

supportingContext: The new flow artifact grounds the real kill transition in `/Users/clive/.factory/missions/69792bf9-59b0-4933-a873-43c546853bc1/progress_log.jsonl` and `state.json`, which record `worker_failed(reason = "Killed by user")` followed by `mission_paused` on the same selected Mission session rather than relying on screenshot-only paused-state evidence.

The selected Electron session is not a wrapper around a different mission id: `/tmp/droi-mission-e2e/sessions/69792bf9-59b0-4933-a873-43c546853bc1.json` uses the same session id as `missionBaseSessionId` and points directly at the persisted missionDir for the real killed Mission.
The broader `.factory/validation/mission-verification/feature-owned-proof-followup.json` bundle remains supporting context only and is no longer the owner for VAL-CROSS-005.

proofOwnershipNotes: This file is the new checked-in feature-owned deliverable for `mission-cross-flow-kill-worker-pause-proof`.
It intentionally narrows ownership to VAL-CROSS-005 instead of relying on the superseded combined follow-up proof bundle.
The Mission-GUI-TEST evidence cited here stays on the Electron surface with the required isolated app-data directory and Custom `CCH-GPT-5.4` model selection.
